skill.name: role-creator
skill.description: Create and install Codex custom agent roles in ~/.codex/config.toml, generate role config files, enforce supported keys, and guide users through required role inputs (model, reasoning effort, developer_instructions).
skill.summary: Create and manage custom Codex/Claude Code agent roles with validation against the official config schema.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: All 225 static findings are false positives. The skill performs legitimate Codex configuration management. Shell command substitutions use parameterized queries with trusted CLI tools (tomlq, jq). Filesystem access is limited to the standard ~/.codex/config.toml location. The flagged 'cryptographic algorithm' patterns are actually Codex config values (reasoning effort levels). No actual security risks identified.

content.value_statement: This skill helps users create, install, and validate custom agent roles for Codex/Claude Code by generating properly structured config files and registering them in the main configuration.
actual_capabilities: Generate role config files with required fields (model, reasoning effort, developer instructions)
Validate role configs against the official Codex config schema
Support optional controls like sandbox mode, web search, and MCP server configurations
Create backups before modifying existing configurations
limitations: Requires Codex/Claude Code to be installed on the system
Schema validation requires access to codex-rs/core/config.schema.json
Does not support creating roles for other AI assistants
Project-scoped roles require .codex directory to exist in the project

use_cases.title: Create a Research Subagent
use_cases.description: Set up a read-only researcher role that can analyze code and documentation without making changes
use_cases.target_user: Developers who want specialized agents for code research

use_cases.title: Build a Code Review Agent
use_cases.description: Create a dedicated code reviewer agent with specific guidelines for feedback and analysis
use_cases.target_user: Team leads who need consistent code review workflows

use_cases.title: Configure Restricted Sandbox Roles
use_cases.description: Create sandboxed agent roles with limited filesystem access for safe experimentation
use_cases.target_user: Security-conscious developers or teams with strict access policies

prompt_templates.title: Basic Role Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the role-creator skill to create a new Codex role called 'researcher' with model gpt-5.3-codex, reasoning effort medium, and developer instructions for reading code only. Install it globally.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a simple read-only research agent

prompt_templates.title: Restricted Role with Sandbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sandboxed code writer role using role-creator. Use model gpt-5.3-codex, high reasoning, and set sandbox_mode to workspace-write with network_access false.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a constrained write-agent for safe code modifications

prompt_templates.title: Project-Scoped Role
prompt_templates.prompt: Use role-creator to add a 'frontend-specialist' role to my current project's .codex/config.toml. Use the full-role-config.toml template as a base and customize for React development.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a specialized role to a specific project

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Role
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the existing 'reviewer' role in my global Codex config to use higher reasoning effort and new developer instructions for security-focused reviews.

output_examples.output:
Installed role 'researcher' in ~/.codex/config.toml
Role config file: ~/.codex/agents/researcher.toml
Backup created at: ~/.codex/config.toml.bak.20240315123045

Configured now: model, model_reasoning_effort, developer_instructions

Spawn example: {"agent_type":"researcher","message":"Find all uses of the auth function"}

Role validation passed for 'researcher'.
- [agents.researcher] declaration: valid
- Required keys present: model, model_reasoning_effort, developer_instructions
- Top-level keys: validated against schema

best_practices: Start with minimal role configs and only add optional parameters when explicitly needed
Always validate roles against the Codex schema before reporting success to users
Use project-scoped roles for team-shared configurations and global roles for personal preferences
anti_patterns: Setting sandbox permissions without explicit user request - this grants elevated privileges by default
Skipping validation - always verify role configs against the official schema
Creating roles with vague developer instructions - be specific about scope and boundaries

faq.question: What is the difference between global and project-scoped roles?
faq.answer: Global roles are installed in ~/.codex/config.toml and available in all projects. Project-scoped roles go in .codex/config.toml within a specific project and only work within that project's context.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code instead of Codex?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Codex but the configuration patterns are similar. Claude Code uses a different config structure, so some features may not apply.

faq.question: How do I enable MCP servers in my role?
faq.answer: Use the --mcp-enable or --mcp-disable flags, or start from a template in the templates/ folder and manually add your MCP server configuration.

faq.question: What happens if I try to create a role that already exists?
faq.answer: The installer will reject the operation unless you pass the --update-existing flag. This prevents accidental overwrites of existing role configurations.

faq.question: Do I need to install Codex to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill manages Codex configuration files. Without Codex installed, the config files would be created but cannot be validated against the schema.

faq.question: How do I create a read-only research role?
faq.answer: Use the minimal template or specify sandbox_mode = read-only in your role config. This prevents the agent from making any filesystem changes.
